title: Dutra Zeneta
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Character
|type=Rebel

|name=Dutra Zeneta
|homeworld=
|birth=Between [[74 BBY]]–[[70 BBY/Legends|70 BBY]]<ref name="DZ-age">Dutra Zeneta's age is given as 74 in the "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]" section of [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 10]]. As the [[Time/Legends|time]] frame for the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' is set during [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], an exact [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] [[Standard year/Legends|year]] cannot be determined.</ref>
|death=
|species=[[Jin]]<ref name="Shroud">{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)}}</ref>
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]<ref name="Shroud" />

{{Quote|The Shroud Team has been a blessing to those agents in the Divis Arm in need of Foster Agents. Agent Tynan's recent assignment in the region turned for the worse when some of her sources proved unreliable, and only through the efforts of Lieutenant Zeneta and his team did she survive.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="Shroud" />}}
'''Dutra Zeneta''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jin]] from the [[Zchtek worlds]] who joined the growing resistance that became the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] shortly after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Subjugation of the Zchtek worlds|subjugated]] his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. A decorated and highly skilled [[Commando/Legends|commando]] and [[Infiltrators/Legends|infiltrator]], Zeneta participated in some of the Alliance's most perilous missions, including the [[Vidicx Campaign|Vidicx]] and [[Loac Campaign]]s, and was a member of the elite [[Scandium Team]], leading [[Unit Theta]]. One of only six Scandium Team survivors from the [[Wellte-ir Massacre]], Zeneta suffered from recurring [[Sleep/Legends|nightmares]] and depression as a result of the [[Ground warfare/Legends|battle]], which resulted in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of fifteen operatives out of the twenty-one deployed.

After completing his twentieth assignment for [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Zeneta faced retirement or a desk job, as was standard procedure. Instead, he petitioned [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] to grant him a posting as a [[Foster Agent]] responsible for assisting stranded Alliance [[Agent/Legends|operatives]] to return to the Alliance and to give him command of a whole team of Foster Agents, a previously unheard of concept. High Command agreed to his request, and the all-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Shroud Team]] was formed. Zeneta and his unit were inserted into [[Corint City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pirik]] in the [[Divis Arm/Legends|Divis Arm]], where they successfully returned sixteen agents to the Alliance in the space of seven [[Standard month/Legends|standard months]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|The modified communication modules have proven very valuable in several key areas where we have planted espionage droids. The module with which Lieutenant Zeneta confers has provided his unit with some very valuable information. With the data, we have been successful in capturing two command frigates along the Sisar Run.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="Field" />}}
===Early service and Scandium Team===
Dutra Zeneta was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jin]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]]<ref name="Shroud" /> sometime between [[74 BBY]] and [[70 BBY/Legends|70 BBY]].<ref name="DZ-age" /> When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Subjugation of the Zchtek worlds|subjugated]] the [[Zchtek worlds]]—home of the Jin [[Species/Legends|species]]—in the early [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] reign, Zeneta left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and, along with many of his fellow Jin, joined the fledgling resistance that eventually became the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="Shroud" />

Trained as an [[Infiltrators/Legends|infiltrator]] under the auspices of [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]], Zeneta was assigned to [[Scandium Team]], an elite unit that at its peak consisted of twenty-eight operatives divided into four teams. The unit was based in the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]] but was deployed across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as required. In command of [[Unit Theta]], a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of crack [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] with both a high success and [[Death/Legends|mortality]] rate, Zeneta fought in and survived some of the most perilous combat missions that the Alliance undertook during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He participated in both the [[Vidicx Campaign|Vidicx]] and [[Loac Campaign]]s as well as the [[Wellte-ir Massacre]], the mission that proved to be Scandium Team's final assignment.<ref name="Shroud" />

During the mission to the [[Wellte-ir system/Legends|Wellte-ir system]]<ref name="Scandium">{{JournalCite|11|Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)}}</ref> in the [[Trax sector/Legends|Trax sector]],<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Scandium Team was engaged by two units of elite Imperial [[Armor/Legends|armor]], one of which was later suspected to be a contingent of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] on field exercises. Heavily outnumbered, Scandium Team fought a four-[[Standard day/Legends|day]] [[Ground warfare/Legends|battle]] on the plains of [[Bresnan]]. Out of twenty-one deployed operatives, fifteen of them died by the [[Time/Legends|time]] the team was extracted from along the [[Umber Banks]].<ref name="Scandium" /> Zeneta was one of only six survivors,<ref name="Shroud" /> along with team leader [[Harovan Toth]], infiltrator [[Korgath]], heavy weapons specialist [[Opit-Wenbruh]], [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Aven Cholus]], and desert [[Wilderness Fighters|Wilderness Fighter]] [[Rith Tar'ak]]. Scandium Team was disbanded,<ref name="Scandium" /> and the loss of so many of his friends traumatized the Jin, leading him to suffer from recurring [[Sleep/Legends|nightmares]] and bouts of depression. Despite the psychological trauma incurred, Zeneta continued to serve the Alliance, wholly believing in its goals.<ref name="Shroud" />

===Shroud Team===
Sometime after [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Dating">The time frame for the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' is set from [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Zeneta completed his twentieth mission for Alliance Intelligence and was faced with retirement or a desk job,<ref name="Shroud" /> as was standard procedure for [[Agent/Legends|agents]] who had survived twenty missions.<ref name="RASB">[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref> Instead, he requested a posting as a [[Foster Agent]],<ref name="Shroud" /> one of the most dangerous field assignments in the Alliance. Foster Agents were used as points of contact for operatives who had been stranded in the field or who had been exposed, giving them a means of returning to the Alliance. Foster Agents ran the risk of being uncovered themselves, as Alliance operatives who had been supplied their name could have divulged it to Imperial forces if captured.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> Zeneta not only convinced [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] to accede to his request but to grant him command of an entire team of Agents as well—a concept of which had previously been unheard, as Foster Agents usually worked alone. Due to the dangerous nature of the work, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vernan (general)|Vernan]],<ref name="Shroud" /> then-[[Chief of Intelligence/Legends|Chief of Alliance Intelligence]],<ref name="RASB" /> suspected that Zeneta had a death wish.<ref name="Shroud" />

Zeneta, holding the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], formed [[Shroud Team]], which was composed entirely of [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]]. The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Dheendo]] served as the unit's primary contact Foster Agent and Zeneta's [[Executive officer/Legends|second-in-command]]. [[Thilis-Brin]], an [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]], functioned as the team's [[Technician/Legends|technician]], while [[Tamo Lan]], a [[Carosite]], provided [[Medic/Legends|medical]] services. [[Gondara]], an [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] teenager, served as Lan's assistant. Shroud Team was inserted into [[Corint City]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pirik]] in the [[Divis Arm/Legends|Divis Arm]]. Corint [[City/Legends|City]] was a fertile recruiting ground for new Alliance operatives and sympathizers and was often used by Alliance operatives seeking refuge. In addition, the population of Corint City was largely alien, with [[Human/Legends|Humans]] only representing around 5 percent of the settlement's 10 million residents. This allowed the all-alien Shroud Team to blend in with the local population more easily. In the first seven [[Standard month/Legends|standard months]] of its deployment—equating to eleven local months—Shroud Team successfully returned sixteen agents, including [[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] Agent [[Tynan Ryln Mei]], to the Alliance. Shroud Team's success rate earned it a great deal of respect from other Foster Agents. In addition to its primary mission, Shroud Team also acquired provisions and equipment for the Alliance. Some the team kept for its own operations, others were for the use of the operatives whom they returned to active duty, and the rest was distributed to other Alliance units operating in the Divis Arm.<ref name="Shroud" />

Zeneta often conversed with an [[espionage droid]] fitted with a modified [[TranLang III communications module/Legends|TranLang III communications module]] who used certain predetermined code phrases and words to relay information to a receiving agent. The information passed to Zeneta in this fashion led to the Alliance capturing two command [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] along the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]].<ref name="Field">{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Field Guide: Slicing Aids}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|It is also refreshing to see a veteran of the Scandium Team set aside his pain and memories and use his abundant skill and knowledge to train a new generation of Alliance agents.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="Shroud" />}}
Dutra Zeneta had [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and large, black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that, like all Jin, could see in the [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] spectrum. He had long, black [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that reached his shoulders and a beard that ran along his jaw line and ended in a pointed tuft beneath his chin. At the age of seventy-four, he was considered to be middle-aged for a Jin. A combat veteran, Zeneta's body was covered in scars from near-fatal wounds. He considered himself to be a Rebel above everything else and truly believed in the Alliance's eventual victory over the Galactic Empire. A skilled tactician and leader, Zeneta was well respected by those under his command and his superiors, and he was a highly decorated operative.<ref name="Shroud" />

A survivor of the Wellte-ir Massacre, Zeneta experienced recurring nightmares about the event and suffered from bouts of depression that affected his abilities if he let them overwhelm him. Like all other Scandium Team survivors, he often displayed a cynical attitude and developed a short temper. Zeneta rarely worked with other Scandium Team survivors due to the shared sense of loss and pain they all felt, and he preferred to spend his free time alone. Despite this, Zeneta was generally regarded to be a warm individual who treated those under his command well, a trait that extended to those operatives he was tasked with returning to the Alliance.<ref name="Shroud" />

Zeneta had a wealth of experience from his [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of service and often related the lessons he learned from his previous adventures to Shroud Team when its members encountered situations similar to those faced by Scandium Team. He had a policy of openly sharing advice, as he knew he had a lot of knowledge to impart. General [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], head of Alliance Intelligence, was pleased that Zeneta was able to put aside his personal grief and assist in the training of new Alliance operatives, giving them the benefit of his experiences.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Skills and abilities==
A skilled and physically fit commando, Dutra Zeneta was trained in both [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee]] and [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]], and he was proficient with the use of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], with a specialty in wielding heavy [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]]. He was also skilled in the use of [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] and explosives for demolition purposes, and he could operate weapons mounted on vehicles, [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], and [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]]. Zeneta was trained in [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]] and could pilot transports and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], most notably the [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]]. He could drive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles and [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bikes; operate [[Walker/Legends|walkers]], including [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]]; and ride animals, such as the [[Cracian thumper]]. Zeneta was also trained in the use of [[Power armor/Legends|powersuits]].<ref name="Shroud" />

Technically adept, Zeneta could operate [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]], security, and communications equipment as well as the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] systems aboard space transports. He was also adept at repairing and programming both [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and [[Computer/Legends|computers]]. In addition, he could repair repulsorlift vehicles, transports, and [[Starship/Legends|ship]]-mounted weapons. The Jin was knowledgeable about alien species, cultures, and [[Language/Legends|languages]] and about the way [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracies]] were run. He was well versed in [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] protocols and was well informed about Corint City's inner workings.<ref name="Shroud" />

An able [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], Zeneta was respected by both his subordinates and superiors. He was a skilled forger, and his combat training allowed him to be stealthy when the need arose. Trained in first-aid, Zeneta was also adept at surviving in inhospitable conditions, and he specialized in urban environment survival.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Equipment==
During his deployment to Corint City, Zeneta generally wore civilian [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]]<ref name="Shroud" />—which included a blue padded vest with furred collar, white [[shirt]], and [[Limb/Legends|fingerless]] black [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]<ref name="Featured Artist" />—and a headset [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and he armed himself with a modified heavy blaster [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] and a pair of grenades. Despite his trauma over the sundering of Scandium Team, he kept a [[Holopic/Legends|holo]] of its members.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dutra Zeneta first appeared in "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]," an article series that appeared in issues of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', published by [[West End Games]]. Zeneta was featured in the article published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|tenth issue]], released in [[1996]], which was written by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]]. A black-and-white illustration of Zeneta by [[Kathy Burdette]] accompanied his biography. A second article in the issue, "[[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide: Slicing Aids]]," namechecked Zeneta in its entry for a modified TranLang III communications module. The article was also written by Carey. In the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|following issue]], a colorized version of Burdette's image was included in the "[[Featured Artist: Kathy Burdette|Featured Artist]]" section.
